tweets.text:
Again: Strategy can easily raise more money with more common ATM sales (they’ve already issued the capacity for up to another $26B)

What that doesn’t mean is that its value additive to MSTR shareholders.

One of the main goals of STRC was to fund BTC purchases that would be less dilutive.

Now - people are justifying ATM common sales of MSTR at mNAV levels Saylor himself said weren’t conducive for increasing Sats/share to fund STRC dividend payments.

It’s obvious the common shares are going to continue to pay the yield for the preferred shares.

Why would anyone want to hold MSTR instead of Bitcoin?
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Strategy's annual dividend obligations are $1.712B. That's $143M/mo.

Strategy routinely raises more than that in less than a day with the common ATM.

Scenarios where Saylor doesn't have enough capital markets access to raise $143M/mo until the next BTC bull begins are remote.

thread_tweets.text:
May 2013: "In this paper we propose Zerocoin, a cryptographic extension to Bitcoin that augments the protocol to allow for fully anonymous currency transactions."

You would know, this was before any altcoins had real success - absurd to suggest this was some sort of feint & not sincere.

Satoshi effectively instamined 5% of Bitcoin. If Bitcoin's community wasn't going to help them, there is no reason a development team like zec/eth should work for you for free.

tweets.text:
a couple people asked, here's the closeout command i created.  i do this anytime i finish working on something significant.

Close out this session:
- update Current Focus
- record important Decisions
- update Source Trail
- refresh Next Actions
- save deliverables in outputs/
- update hot-cache.md
- update active-sessions.md
- make sure the final response includes file paths
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
The AI setup that has been working for me:

- Obsidian as the brain
- Mac mini as the home base
- Tailscale + SSH + tmux so sessions survive disconnects
- A closeout command after real work so the system remembers what changed
tweets.full_text:
a couple people asked, here's the closeout command i created.  i do this anytime i finish working on something significant.

Close out this session:
- update Current Focus
- record important Decisions
- update Source Trail
- refresh Next Actions
- save deliverables in outputs/
- update hot-cache.md
- update active-sessions.md
- make sure the final response includes file paths
